title: BCI Competition 2020 Track 4 — Upper-limb grasping motor imagery
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 15 healthy participants performing motor imagery of three upper-limb grasping tasks (cylindrical, spherical, and lumbrical) across three sessions separated by one week. Recorded at 250 Hz from 60 channels using a standard 10-05 montage, the dataset contains 6,750 trials total and was designed to evaluate session-to-session transfer learning in brain-computer interface applications. This is a derivative dataset created from the BCI Competition 2020 Track 4 data, preprocessed with a 60 Hz notch filter and epoched to extract the motor imagery window.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 250 Hz from 60 channels using a BrainAmp amplifier (BrainProducts GmbH) with a standard 10-05 montage, referenced to FCz with ground at Fpz. Participants performed cue-based motor imagery of three grasping tasks in response to visual cues. Each trial consisted of a 3-second rest period, 3-second visual cue, and 4-second motor imagery window. Data were preprocessed with a 60 Hz notch filter and epoched to extract the motor imagery window.
